title: Gallai's Conjecture
statement_text: Every connected graph on $n$ vertices can be decomposed into $\lceil \frac{n}{2} \rceil$ paths.
context_text: In 1968, Gallai conjectured that the edges of any connected graph on $n$ vertices can be partitioned into $\lceil n/2 \rceil$ paths. The conjecture remains open in general and had previously been confirmed only for specific graph classes such as graphs with maximum degree at most 5, series-parallel graphs, planar 3-trees, and triangle-free planar graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves Gallai's conjecture for the entire class of planar graphs (Theorem 1.1): every connected planar graph on $n$ vertices can be decomposed into $\lceil n/2 \rceil$ paths.
paper_title: Gallai's path decomposition in planar graphs

title: Bonamy–Perrett Question 1.1
statement_text: Does every connected graph on $n$ vertices that is not an odd semi-clique admit a $\lfloor \frac{n}{2} \rfloor$-path decomposition?
context_text: Bonamy and Perrett asked in 2016 whether one can always save one path in the decomposition when $n$ is odd, for graphs that are not odd semi-cliques. An odd semi-clique is a graph obtained from a clique on $2k+1$ vertices by deleting at most $k-1$ edges.
paper_contribution: Answers the question positively for planar graphs (Theorem 1.2): every connected planar graph on $n$ vertices, except $K_3$ and $K_5^-$ (the only two planar odd semi-cliques), can be decomposed into $\lfloor n/2 \rfloor$ paths.
paper_title: Gallai's path decomposition in planar graphs
